summary: Create minimal M7 Beta content and quality task graph
acceptance_criteria: Create a minimal, dependency-ordered M7 task DAG under project/tasks/active with explicit owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, dependencies, allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance_criteria, required_tests, route_to, and notes for every M7 task.

The M7 DAG covers Roadmap and acceptance-matrix M7 goals without implementing them: content lock planning, historical/cultural/language review, full scenario/person/event fill path, large-scale balance and anti-snowball validation, performance/memory/loading validation, Chrome/Edge/Firefox/Windows and resolution/localization matrix, save migration and cloud-save candidate decision boundaries, tutorial/hints/encyclopedia completion, audio/art/localization completion planning, Beta culture review, and manual playthrough evidence.

The M7 DAG explicitly preserves M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS and M5 manual node battle decision DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE: no manual node battle implementation task, no M7/M8 blocking manual node battle spike, and no manual node battle research dependency may be created.

The M7 DAG preserves Human Gates for major cultural-risk decisions, formal content lock acceptance, manual playthrough acceptance where human judgment is required, frozen product/platform/core architecture changes, paid services, accounts/secrets, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial decisions, branding, irreversible migrations, and any formal 1.0 scope reversal.
Tasks are small enough for independent writer/reviewer routing, with no overlapping writer ownership for the same package, content, platform, or document region.

No product code, package code, content-source data, roadmap edits, acceptance-matrix edits, accepted ADR changes, implementation work, production dependency changes, release artifacts, telemetry, networking, or mod support is performed by this planning task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead acceptance.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for all new M7 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape

qa_reviewer.next_action: Review M7 task graph and emit ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK handoff to lead_orchestrator.

notes: M7 starts only after M6-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 is CLOSED on origin/main.

This task creates or restores the M7 task graph only; it must not implement content, balance changes, UI, platform matrix behavior, save migration, release artifacts, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, or manual node battle.
M6 Gate PASS_WITH_LIMITS carries forward bounded Alpha/local evidence limits and must not be re-described as unconditional release readiness.
Manual node battle remains deferred from M5 and the 1.0 mainline; do not create an implementation task or blocking spike/research task for M7 or M8.
Major cultural-risk decisions and formal content lock acceptance remain Human Gates; task graph may route research/review tasks but must not silently approve them.
M7 task graph should leave only the first dependency-ready downstream task READY after review; other downstream M7 tasks should remain DRAFT until dependencies are satisfied.
Created a dependency-ordered M7 Beta content and quality DAG with M7-CONTENT-LOCK-CONTRACT-001 as the only first downstream READY task after graph acceptance; all other downstream tasks are DRAFT.
The DAG preserves M6_GATE = PASS_WITH_LIMITS and does not describe M6 as unconditional release readiness.
The DAG preserves DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE exactly and contains no manual node battle implementation task or M7/M8 blocking spike/research/validation dependency.

Formal content lock acceptance, manual playthrough acceptance, major cultural risk, commercial/release/branding, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save implementation, arbitrary-code mods, irreversible migration, and frozen product/platform/core changes remain Human Gates.

No product code, package code, content-source data, roadmap, acceptance matrix, prior gate, accepted ADR, CI, tool, release artifact, telemetry, server/multiplayer, mod, cloud, or manual battle work was performed.
next_action: Await independent qa_reviewer verdict for M7 task graph.

coverage: save migration and cloud-save candidate decision boundaries
